name: Nezha Prime
description: Behold: the Scion of the Burning Wind, now before you in most courtly accoutrement. Featuring altered mod polarities for greater customization.

abilities.name: Fire Walker
abilities.description: Blaze a trail of flames, scorching enemies and cleansing allies. Teleporting blasts the landing area with a ring of fire.

abilities.name: Blazing Chakram
abilities.description: Hurl a flaming ring that sets enemies ablaze making them vulnerable to any damage. Flaming enemies drop Restorative Orbs on death. Charge to amplify the power of the ring, and reactivate to instantly travel to the ring's location.

abilities.name: Warding Halo
abilities.description: Create a protective ring of fire, that also stuns and damages enemies who get too close.

abilities.name: Divine Spears
abilities.description: Impale nearby enemies on spears that erupt from the below. Activate again to slam surviving enemies back into the ground.

components.name: Blueprint
components.description: Behold: the Scion of the Burning Wind, now before you in most courtly accoutrement. Featuring altered mod polarities for greater customization.

components.name: Chassis
components.description: Chassis component of the Nezha Prime Warframe.

components.name: Neuroptics
components.description: Neuroptics component of the Nezha Prime Warframe.

components.name: Orokin Cell
components.description:
Ancient energy cell from the Orokin era.

Location: Ceres, Saturn, and Deimos

components.name: Systems
components.description: Systems component of the Nezha Prime Warframe.

patchlogs.name: Update 32.2.0: Lua’s Prey
patchlogs.changes:
Enemies captured in Sea Snares now have 200% Damage Vulnerability from all other sources (similar to Nezha’s Blazing Chakram). 
Similar to how Nezha’s Divine Spears impales and slams enemies in two separate actions, Riptide now pulls in enemies first and explodes in the next.

patchlogs.name: Mask of the Revenant: Hotfix 23.8.2
patchlogs.fixes:
Fixed Nezha’s Blazing Chakram having a Critical Multiplier of 0.

patchlogs.name: Mask of the Revenant: Update 23.8.0 + 23.8.0.1 + 23.8.0.2
patchlogs.changes: Changed Nezha’s Fire Walker to only ‘hop’ when cast while on the floor.
patchlogs.fixes:
Making Nezha feel smoother and more fluid in gameplay
Increasing Nezha’s power overall by giving him added team support value and internal synergies
Why? Adds great team value to Nezha’s kit 
Why? When considering Nezha’s revamped kit, he is excellent at mitigating enemy damage 
With 90% damage resistance, Nezha is still very capable of tanking, but encouraged to rely on his other tools to avoid getting overwhelmed. Taking minimal health damage allows for synergy with Blazing Chakram’s health orbs, not to mention new modding avenues like Equilibrium, Health Conversion and various Arcanes. The change also allows us to improve survivability in other ways, such as the increased health pool, and major Warding Halo quality-of-life buffs listed below.
(Brief aside: as a Nezha main, I was originally skeptical of the 90% damage resistance change, and I suspect many readers will be skeptical too. However, playing the rework myself quickly changed my mind. The various buffs really outweigh the negatives, making Nezha much more capable in a supporting crowd-controller role. If you doubt just how potent 90% damage resistance can be, try out Gara’s Splinter Storm at 130% or more power strength!)
On top of all that, Nezha’s sounds have been remastered, adding new auditory cues for important moments, like Blazing Chakrams returning to the player, or Warding Halos running out of health!
Players will receive a HUD icon indicating the Health of a Safeguard Halo they received from Nezha.
Fixed a crash when casting and teleporting with Nezha’s Blazing Chakram ability.
Fixed a crash when casting Nezha’s Warding Halo ability.
Fixed another crash from casting Nezha’s Warding Halo ability.
Fixed a crash when casting Nezha’s Divine Spears ability.

patchlogs.name: Oberon Prime: Hotfix 20.7.1
patchlogs.changes: The Health Conversion Mod now activates upon taking damage instead of getting hit. Previously, Warframes (like Valkyr, Rhino or Nezha) that have abilities that make themselves invincible to damage, were consuming the conversion when hit even though no damage was being done due to invincibility. This rendered the Health Conversion Mod rather useless, and has since been changed to improve functionality.

patchlogs.name: Update 18.1.0
patchlogs.changes: Sortie Season 2 is now live!  Keep an eye on the Sortie menu in the World State Window to observe changes made to the Sortie Reward Pool. We realize this is 2 days ahead of schedule, but we wanted to give everyone a chance to get Nezha starting today!
patchlogs.fixes: Please Note: Nezha parts obtained from Sorties are tradeable, and will be a permanent reward from Sorties in the future.
